skill.name: longbridge-intel
skill.description: Market intelligence skill for Longbridge. It supports stock screeners, rankings, anomalies, constituents, ETF flows, sector rotation, catalysts, supply-chain analysis, and thematic research.
skill.summary: Prompt-only market intelligence hub for Longbridge screeners, rankings, ETF flows, sector rotation, catalysts, and thematic research.
skill.author: longbridge

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported 308 issues, including shell backticks, weak cryptography, reconnaissance, and one URL. Review found markdown CLI examples and finance terminology, not executable code, cryptography, or host reconnaissance. The skill is prompt-only and safe to publish with a low warning for external CLI and network data-source use.

low_findings.title: Documented Longbridge CLI Invocation
low_findings.description: The static shell-execution hits are markdown examples for Longbridge CLI commands. They are not scripts or hidden execution paths, but they can cause agent-run external commands when the user requests data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Direct review shows markdown code fences and inline command examples only. No script file, spawn call, eval call, or user-input command concatenation was found.

low_findings.title: Documented Network Data Source
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL is the Longbridge MCP endpoint listed as a secondary data source. It is not a covert destination, but users should expect network lookups when CLI data is unavailable.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL appears in a data-source priority list with CLI, MCP, and Web Search. No credential transfer or hidden request logic is present in the skill files.

low_findings.title: False Positive Weak-Cryptography Matches
low_findings.description: The high-severity weak-cryptography hits are finance and research terms, such as public blockchains, block trades, and ARK methodology. No cryptographic API or weak algorithm use was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Reviewed context confirms these lines describe market-analysis concepts. There is no MD5, SHA1, cipher setup, password handling, or cryptographic implementation.

low_findings.title: False Positive System-Reconnaissance Matches
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance hits refer to market research, sector scans, and market microstructure fields. They do not inspect the host system, filesystem, users, processes, or network interfaces.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are output fields and finance workflow text. No OS discovery commands or host inventory behavior were found.

content.value_statement: Market research is fragmented across screeners, rankings, news, flows, and sector data. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code one Longbridge workflow for structured market intelligence.
actual_capabilities: Run Longbridge strategy screeners and custom indicator filters across supported markets.
Show popularity rankings, top movers, quote anomalies, and related news context.
Analyze index and ETF constituents, tracking error, liquidity, premium, and discount.
Generate morning briefings from overnight moves, watchlist catalysts, and market events.
Compare ETF flows, sector rotation, and supply-chain links for thematic research.
Produce ARK-style disruptive innovation diagnostics with scenario assumptions and disclaimers.
limitations: It is read-only and does not place trades or manage orders.
It depends on the Longbridge CLI, MCP, or Web Search being available at runtime.
Some metrics require current CLI flags, so the agent must check help output first.
Its research output is analysis support, not financial advice.

use_cases.title: Pre-market briefing
use_cases.description: Summarize overnight moves, watchlist catalysts, and upcoming events before the trading day.

use_cases.title: Sector rotation review
use_cases.description: Compare sector strength, ETF flows, valuations, and constituent momentum across markets.

use_cases.title: Event-driven monitoring
use_cases.description: Track earnings, buybacks, management changes, and index events across a watchlist.

prompt_templates.title: Screen value stocks
prompt_templates.prompt: Screen Hong Kong stocks with P/E between 8 and 20 and ROE above 10%. Show the top 20 results.

prompt_templates.title: Create a morning brief
prompt_templates.prompt: Give me a morning brief for NVDA.US, 700.HK, and 600519.SH. Include moves, catalysts, and events for today.

prompt_templates.title: Compare sector rotation
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare US technology, China electric vehicles, and Hong Kong financials using ETF flows and sector strength.

prompt_templates.title: Model innovation scenarios
prompt_templates.prompt: Run an ARK-style disruptive innovation analysis for TSLA.US with TAM, cost curve, and three five-year scenarios.

output_examples.input: Give me a morning brief for NVDA.US, 700.HK, and 600519.SH.
output: Overnight moves: NVDA.US rose on AI chip demand, 700.HK was soft, and 600519.SH was flat.
Catalysts: Tencent buyback news is active. No major Maotai catalyst was found.
Today: Watch earnings calendar, policy headlines, and market open reaction.

output_examples.input: Compare US technology, China electric vehicles, and Hong Kong financials.
output: US technology leads on ETF inflows and stronger constituent momentum.
China electric vehicles show mixed flows and uneven stock participation.
Hong Kong financials lag due to weaker flows and broad constituent pressure.

output_examples.input: Analyze SPY.US as an ETF allocation candidate.
output: Product profile: Broad US equity exposure with high liquidity.
Tracking and premium: Review tracking error and premium or discount before sizing.
Allocation fit: Useful as a core market beta position, subject to portfolio overlap.

best_practices: Run the relevant Longbridge help command before relying on CLI flags.
Specify market and ticker suffixes to avoid symbol ambiguity.
Pair screeners with catalyst checks before drawing conclusions.
anti_patterns: Do not treat scenario outputs as guaranteed price targets.
Do not use stale after-hours microstructure data without a caveat.
Do not present the output as personalized financial advice.

faq.question: Does this skill place trades?
faq.answer: No. It is read-only and only supports market data research workflows.

faq.question: Does it require a Longbridge login?
faq.answer: The skill states that its CLI commands use public data and do not require login.

faq.question: Which languages does it support?
faq.answer: It can respond in Simplified Chinese, Traditional Chinese, or English.

faq.question: How is it different from a quote tool?
faq.answer: It combines screeners, rankings, catalysts, ETF flows, sectors, and research frameworks.

faq.question: Can it use Web Search?
faq.answer: Yes, but only as a fallback for data not available from Longbridge sources.

faq.question: Is the ARK-style output official ARK research?
faq.answer: No. It is an independent framework and must include a disclaimer.
